title: drynaria serpens
intro: Drynaria serpens, also known as the adder's tongue fern or adder's tongue, is a type of epiphytic fern native to tropical Asia. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating conditions such as rheumatism, arthritis, and snake bites. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, and promoting wound healing. Drynaria serpens can be prepared into various herbal forms, including capsules, tablets, and teas, and is often used in combination with other medicinal plants to enhance its therapeutic effects.
